title: Cat Hair Loss in Circular Patches: Causes & Veterinary Advice
meta_desc: Learn about circular hair loss in cats, common causes like ringworm, why it is usually a low-urgency issue, and how photos assist in diagnosis.
content_html:
What is it?

Circular patches of hair loss, also known as focal alopecia, are a common dermatological concern in felines. This pattern is frequently associated with ringworm, which is actually a fungal infection rather than a parasite. Other potential causes include localized allergic reactions, external parasites like mites or fleas, or psychogenic alopecia where a cat over-grooms a specific spot due to stress. In some cases, a bacterial skin infection (pyoderma) may also present with circular thinning or loss of fur.

Is it an emergency?

The urgency level for circular hair loss is generally low. While these patches can be itchy or uncomfortable for your pet, they are rarely life-threatening. You should schedule a routine appointment with your veterinarian to identify the underlying cause and prevent the condition from spreading to other parts of the body or other pets. However, if the area appears severely infected, is oozing, or if your cat is showing signs of systemic illness like lethargy or loss of appetite, you should seek a more prompt evaluation.

Why a photo helps triage

Taking a clear, well-lit photo of the affected area is a vital step in the triage process. A photograph allows your veterinary team to assess the appearance of the skin—checking for redness, scaling, crusting, or broken hair shafts—without immediately stressing the cat. These visual cues help the vet prioritize your appointment and prepare the necessary diagnostic tools, such as a Wood's lamp or skin scraping kit, before you even arrive at the clinic.

faqs.question: Is circular hair loss in cats contagious?
faqs.answer: It can be. If the cause is ringworm or certain mites, it can spread to other pets and even humans. It is best to wash your hands after touching the affected areas until a diagnosis is confirmed.

faqs.question: Can stress cause my cat to lose hair in patches?
faqs.answer: Yes, cats often respond to stress by over-grooming. This behavior can result in clean-shaven-looking circular patches, usually in reachable areas like the belly or legs.

faqs.question: Will the hair grow back?
faqs.answer: In most cases, yes. Once the underlying fungal, parasitic, or behavioral issue is treated, the hair follicles will typically recover and new fur will begin to grow within a few weeks.
